name: Blue Moon MK1
first: First flight of Blue Origin's Blue Moon lunar lander architecture
description: Blue Origin's first cargo Blue Moon lander, contracted under NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) program. A single-stage descent vehicle powered by the BE-7 cryogenic engine, designed to deliver up to ~3 tonnes of payload to the lunar surface. Launches on Blue Origin's New Glenn from Cape Canaveral, performs trans-lunar injection on the New Glenn second stage, then executes lunar orbit insertion and powered descent. The Mark 1 cargo flight precedes the human-rated Mark 2, which is contracted to support NASA's Artemis V crewed lunar landing.

events.label: NEW GLENN LAUNCH
events.note: Lifts off from Cape Canaveral LC-36; first stage returns for downrange recovery.

events.label: TLI
events.note: New Glenn second stage performs trans-lunar injection burn.

events.label: LOI
events.note: Lunar orbit insertion via BE-7 main engine.

events.label: POWERED DESCENT
events.note: BE-7 throttled descent to lunar surface — first Blue Origin lunar touchdown.
